seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Smolagents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Smolagents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Build powerful AI Coding Assistants using Smolagents, Hugging Face's lightweight Python framework for code-based reasoning and tool integration. These agents excel at repository-aware code generation, sandbox execution, and iterative debugging but face risks like unsafe code execution and security vulnerabilities. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify code correctness, security, and compliance before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Smolagents
architectureOverview.description:
The typical AI Coding Assistant with Smolagents follows a code-aware LLM pipeline: a base model (e.g., CodeLlama) receives repository context and user tasks, generates Python code via Smolagents' agent.run(), executes it in a sandbox, validates against tests, and iterates on failures. Smolagents' simplicity enables rapid prototyping with tools for file I/O, git operations, and testing frameworks like pytest.

Key components include: (1) Context retriever loading repo files/tree, (2) Smolagent with code generation tools, (3) Sandbox executor (Docker/subprocess), (4) Test validator, and (5) Feedback loop for debugging. This creates a self-improving coding agent but amplifies risks from unverified generated code.

Vex integration wraps agent.run() to verify outputs against code correctness, security scans, credential checks, and dependency safety before execution.

risks.name: Unsafe Python Execution
risks.description: Agents generate code with syscalls, file overwrites, or eval() that could delete repos or access system resources.
risks.mitigation: Vex scans for dangerous APIs and enforces sandbox policies before execution.

risks.name: Security Vulnerabilities
risks.description: Generated code includes SQL injection, command injection, or hardcoded secrets from repo context.
risks.mitigation: Vex performs static analysis and credential leakage detection on all outputs.

risks.name: Dependency Safety Issues
risks.description: Agents add malicious packages or vulnerable dependencies via pip installs in generated code.
risks.mitigation: Vex validates dependency trees against known vulnerability databases.

risks.name: License Compliance Violations
risks.description: Code copies unlicensed snippets or mixes incompatible licenses from training data.
risks.mitigation: Vex checks generated code against license databases and flags violations.

implementation.title: Basic Smolagents Code Assistant Setup
implementation.description: Initialize a Smolagents code agent with repository context and code generation tools.
implementation.code:
from smolagents import CodeAgent, Tool

from vex import verify

# Define code generation tools
code_tools = [
    Tool(name="write_file", description="Write code to file"),
    Tool(name="run_tests", description="Execute pytest")
]

# Initialize agent
agent = CodeAgent(
    model="codellama/CodeLlama-7b-hf",
    tools=code_tools,
    system_prompt="You are a secure coding assistant."
)

def coding_assistant(task: str, repo_path: str) -> str:
    context = f"Repo: {repo_path}. Task: {task}"
    return agent.run(context)

implementation.title: Repository Context Loader
implementation.description: Load relevant files and git state for the agent to reason over repo structure.

implementation.title: Sandbox Code Executor
implementation.description: Execute generated code in isolated Docker container with resource limits.
implementation.code:
import docker

def execute_sandbox(code: str, timeout: int = 30) -> dict:
    client = docker.from_env()
    
    with open('/tmp/agent_code.py', 'w') as f:
        f.write(code)
    
    container = client.containers.run(
        'python:3.11-slim',
        command=['python', '/tmp/agent_code.py'],
        detach=True,
        mem_limit='256m',
        network_disabled=True,
        remove=True
    )
    
    result = container.wait(timeout=timeout)
    logs = container.logs().decode()
    
    return {'exit_code': result['StatusCode'], 'output': logs}

implementation.title: Test Suite Validator
implementation.description: Run pytest on generated code changes and validate test coverage.

vexIntegration.title: Wrap agent.run() with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Basic integration verifying code correctness and security before execution.
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify

@verify(
    checks=[
        'code_correctness',
        'security_vulnerabilities',
        'no_credential_leakage'
    ],
    risk_profile='medium'
)
def safe_coding_assistant(task: str, repo_path: str) -> str:
    context = load_repo_context(repo_path)
    response = agent.run(f"Task: {task}\n{context}")
    return response

# Usage
result = safe_coding_assistant(
    "Fix the login bug in auth.py",
    "/path/to/repo"
)
